div1.entryFree: Attorney, substitute, proxy: and will have no a. but myself, Err. V, 100. die by a. As IV, 1, 94. I, by a., bless thee from thy mother, R3 V, 3, 83. Especially one who is appointed to transact business for another, advocate, pleader: the heart's a. (sc. the tongue) Ven. 335. as fit as ten groats is for the hand of an a. All's II, 2, 23. R2 II, 3, 134. H6A V, 3, 166. R3 IV, 4, 127. R3 IV, 4, 127 his --s general == those who are appointed by general authority for all his affairs and suits, R2 II, 1, 203. the king's a. == what is now called attorney-general: H8 II, 1, 15.
